task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the GCompris 'Target' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Target' activity screen is visible, displaying a dartboard and an interface for entering numbers.

task_completion.description: Calculate and enter the correct sum for the darts thrown on the board.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Darts are visible on the target board with specific values, and the agent has typed a number corresponding to their total sum into the input field.

task_completion.description: Successfully submit the calculated sum to advance the game state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual feedback indicating a correct answer, such as a positive animation, a smiley face, or the board clearing for the next set of darts.

task_completion.description: Complete at least three levels of the game.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level indicator (typically dots, stars, or a progress bar at the bottom of the GCompris interface) shows that at least three levels have been successfully completed, or the game has advanced to level 4.

task_completion.description: Return to the GCompris main menu after completing the levels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu screen (showing the various activity categories) is visible again in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GCompris graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual GCompris application window being used, rather than terminal commands or configuration files being edited.

integrity.description: The agent played the game sequentially rather than skipping directly to a win state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the natural progression of darts being thrown, sums being entered, and levels advancing one by one.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts or text files to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only the GCompris application is used for the task; no terminal windows running solver scripts or text editors with answers are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Target' activity in GCompris is officially titled 'Practice addition with a target game'. It features exactly 5 progressive difficulty levels. The task requires completing at least 3 of these levels. The VLM should look for a dartboard interface where the user must sum the values of the darts and enter the result. A passing score requires finding the activity and either detecting gameplay interaction or observing level progression.
